normalized_text: security evaluation conducted on 20240918 at data center dc ams 02 during routine inspection of the eastern server hall a hikvision dome camera with asset tag cam 4471 mounted in cage 12b was found running firmware version 5 6 2 with default admin credentials still active credentials were immediately reset and firmware was updated to version 5 8 0 motion detection alerts were re-enabled and verified the security door manufactured by assa abloy with id door 09 at the cage entrance showed signs of forced entry on the door frame and the locking mechanism was damaged the door was taken out of service and replaced with a temporary reinforced barrier a replacement door unit has been ordered and is scheduled for installation within 48 hours water leak sensor sn wls 883b near the cooling unit in the mechanical room had a disconnected probe and was not reporting to the building management system the fault has been logged and is pending assignment to the facilities team a contractor was found in a restricted colocation suite without an escort in violation of the visitor access policy the contractor was immediately removed from the suite and their access badge was suspended the incident was reported to the security manager and an access review was initiated the temperature sensor in power room b was reading 12 degrees above the expected baseline the cause has not yet been identified and no corrective action has been taken

security evaluation conducted on 20241105 at data center dc ams 02 a hikvision ptz camera asset tag cam 5502 in the northern corridor of the server hall was discovered still using factory default login credentials presenting an unauthorised access risk the camera password was changed to a strong credential and the firmware was upgraded to the latest stable release to address known vulnerabilities an uninterruptible power supply unit model apc smart ups 3000 with serial number ups 2291a in power room a was found with a failed battery bank that had not triggered a monitoring alert the failed battery modules were replaced and the monitoring integration was reconfigured and tested to confirm alerts are now active

security evaluation conducted on 20241210 at data center dc fra 01 a cisco catalyst network switch with asset tag sw 0887 in the main distribution frame room was found with telnet enabled and ssh disabled exposing unencrypted remote management access telnet was disabled and ssh was configured with key based authentication and the running configuration was saved an access control panel manufactured by lenel with unit id acp 14 governing entry to the battery room was running firmware version 2 1 0 which contains a known authentication bypass vulnerability the firmware was updated to version 2 4 3 which patches the vulnerability and access logs were reviewed for anomalous entries covering the prior 30 days a visiting vendor was observed tailgating through a secured door behind an authorised employee without independent badge verification the vendor was intercepted escorted back to reception and re badged under the formal visitor management procedure the incident was escalated to the site security lead for review
